quoted_text:
Brent crude is currently trading in the mid-$70s, with WTI above $70. (Bloomberg data below).
This comes as traders in Asia react to the latest exchange of fire between the US and Iran.
The dominant view in markets is that this latest flare-up will remain contained. While the situation may reoccur periodically, the prevailing belief is that it will not evolve into a broader conflict.
Should this view be challenged in a significant way, however, oil prices could quickly jump into the $80s or $90s
